mnemonics: VIP Edges: Visualize Important Paths Everywhere - A mnemonic for remembering the core components of a graph: Vertices, Edges, and the importance of Paths.
keyPoints: A Graph is a non-linear data structure consisting of vertices (nodes) and edges (connections between nodes).
Graphs can be directed (edges have a direction) or undirected (edges have no direction).
Graphs are used to model networks like social connections, web pages, roads, and dependencies.
commonMistakes: Confusing depth-first search (DFS) and breadth-first search (BFS) traversal algorithms and their appropriate use cases.
Incorrectly assuming a graph is connected or failing to handle disconnected components in algorithms.
